source: Palette after the unmixed tube pigments Josef Albers named on the backs of his 'Homage to the Square' panels (1950-1976); combinations composed after the Homage structure (three or four nested squares - hence 3- and 4-color sets here) and the perception studies in 'Interaction of Color' (1963). Hues are interpretive approximations.

combinations.name: Homage: Yellows
combinations.note: Three yellows nested square in square - the same family made to breathe by nothing but adjacency.

combinations.name: Transparence
combinations.note: The film-color illusion: when the middle color reads as the mixture of its neighbors, the eye invents a transparent layer.

combinations.name: Ascending
combinations.note: After the blue-green Homages: cool colors stacked toward light, each square lifting the next.

combinations.name: Two Look Like One
combinations.note: The famous deception: two different greys on a strong ground, pushed until the eye cannot keep them apart.

combinations.name: Renewed Hope
combinations.note: After the late ochre Homages: earth colors against the dark, warmth persisting.

combinations.name: Homage: Full Square
combinations.note: The four-square format at its sunniest - light pouring inward from white to ochre.

combinations.name: Homage: Reds
combinations.note: Four reds descending into darkness - the same hue family losing light square by square.

combinations.name: Homage: Greens
combinations.note: The green Homages: pale ground, two greens, and blue holding the center.

combinations.name: Grey Steps
combinations.note: The value scale stripped of hue - Albers' first exercise: learn to see lighter and darker before color.

combinations.name: Blues at Dusk
combinations.note: After the nocturnal blue Homages: cool squares sinking toward black, cerulean keeping the last light.
